title: RunLog
description: Immutable log of a complete pipeline run. Records every stage transition, artifact references, costs, and errors. Written by the orchestrator; never modified after a stage completes.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this run. Used as the artifact directory name under runs/artifacts/.

brief_id.description: ID of the ContentBrief that initiated this run.

status.description: Current overall status of the run.

stages.description: Ordered list of stage execution records. One entry per pipeline stage, appended by the orchestrator as each stage is reached.

stage_name.description: Name of the pipeline stage (e.g. intake, research, angles, script, review, output).

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this stage started. Null if stage has not started yet.

finished_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this stage finished. Null if stage has not completed.

input_ref.description: Relative path to the input artifact consumed by this stage (e.g. brief.json). Null if stage has not started or has no file input.

output_ref.description: Relative path to the output artifact produced by this stage (e.g. research.json). Null if stage has not completed.

cost_tokens.description: Total tokens consumed by this stage. Null for stages that do not invoke a language model.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run was started.

finished_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the run completed or failed. Null while the run is still in progress.

total_cost.description: Estimated total cost of the run in USD, summed across all stages. Null while the run is still in progress.

errors.description: List of errors encountered during the run. Empty if no errors occurred.

stage_name.description: Name of the stage where this error occurred.

message.description: Error message describing what went wrong.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this error was recorded.

summary.description: Short human-readable description of the run result. Null while the run is still in progress. Written by the orchestrator upon completion or failure.
